title: On the (855,183,39) Difference-Set Problem in C_3 x C_285
micro_paper_assessment: The packet preserves an exact quotient-rigidity slice for the selected row, but the unresolved C_95 lift still carries the real theorem burden, so this run is below one-shot micro-paper threshold and should be moved aside unless the final contradiction closes quickly.
family_affinity: moderate: prime-order quotient rigidity inside an abelian residual-row problem
generalization_signal: moderate: the divisibility-and-low-norm quotient argument plausibly transfers to rows with similar Frobenius inversion congruences
strongest_honest_claim: Any hypothetical (855,183,39) difference set in C_3 x C_285 must project to C_19 with occupancies 21,9,...,9 and to C_5 with occupancies 27,39,39,39,39, up to translation.
paper_title_hint: Quotient Rigidity for the (855,183,39) Difference-Set Problem in C_3 x C_285
candidate_theorem_slice: Any hypothetical (855,183,39) difference set in C_3 x C_285 must project to C_19 with occupancies 21,9,...,9 and to C_5 with occupancies 27,39,39,39,39, up to translation.
next_action: Preserve the quotient-rigidity slice, but move this packet out of the active micro-paper lane unless a short rigorous contradiction for the induced C_95 lift can be written next without broadening the program.
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize the quotient-rigidity slice; it would not bridge the unresolved C_95 lift or make the packet paper-ready.
